title: narcissus neglectus subsp. grandicrenatus
intro: Narcissus neglectus subsp. grandicrenatus, also known as the Grand Crested Daffodil, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Narcissus neglectus plant, a type of daffodil found in the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Narcissus neglectus subsp. grandicrenatus include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce fever, and even act as an antispasmodic to alleviate muscle cramps and spasms. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used internally to address various health concerns or externally as a topical application to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
